item.title: Why Reverence Cannot Be Programmed
item.description:
Image created as generative AIThe Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowA person steps into an old church. Something shifts before thought catches up. The voice lowers. The step slows. The hands fold without being told. Whatever was said in the parking…

item.title: Competence Without Glory
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowPicture a man we can call Ray.At 3:47 on a Tuesday morning, somewhere in central Ohio, Ray is standing in front of a panel of blinking lights older than his marriage. A pump has failed. He knew it would. He has…

item.title: John Locke and the Ownership of the Self in a Digital Republic
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowPrefaceFew thinkers have shaped the modern political imagination more deeply than John Locke. His arguments about natural rights, government by consent, private property, education, and the limits of political…

item.title: The Illusion of AI Understanding
item.description:
Picture the scene.A user types a question into a glowing rectangle. The rectangle responds. Fluently. Confidently. With the measured cadence of something that has read the relevant literature and arrived at a considered position.The answer is…

item.title: Why Christian Formation Requires Boredom
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowBoredom has become a problem to be solved.Children are entertained from the moment their eyes open. Adults fill every pause with noise. Silence is treated as inefficiency, waiting as failure, stillness as a…

item.title: The Age of the Screenshot
item.description:

item.title: The Confessional Box:

item.title: The Christian Meaning of Saying No
item.description:

item.title: The Professionalization of Outrage
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowSomewhere, right now, someone is angry on your behalf.You may not have asked them to be. You may not know what they are angry about. You may not, upon reflection, be particularly angry yourself. This does not…

item.title: Gladys Ingle: Changing Wheels at Altitude
item.description:

item.title: Prompting Is Not Programming
item.description:
A man sits at his desk, composing instructions for a machine he believes is listening.He types carefully. He uses semicolons. He capitalizes “IMPORTANT” because he has been told the machine responds to emphasis, and he has no way to verify this but…

item.title: Why Christianity Treats Waiting as a Discipline
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowWe are not patient people, and we have built a world that confirms the instinct. Nearly anything can be summoned in minutes. A meal, a car, an answer to a question that once would have required a library and an…

item.title: Utility to Symbolism
item.description:

item.title: The Death of Waiting
item.description:
If you were online in the 1990s, you know the one. The wheeze and screech of a modem negotiating terms with the world. Static that resolved into something almost musical, a rising handshake between your machine and the void. It told you: this costs…

Read the full article on Substack

item.title: The Spiritual Cost of Living Without Ancestors
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowWe live surrounded by information and largely detached from inheritance. We know what is happening everywhere, in real time, across every continent, but many of us have little sense of where we come from. The…

item.title: The Day Hollywood Realized the Camera Was No Longer the Scarce Resource
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowHollywood has spent the better part of a century confusing expense with importance.This is not entirely their fault. For a long time, expense really was importance. Cameras were expensive. Sets were expensive….

item.title: James Watt: Power Without Season
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowHe did not invent steam. He solved the problem that kept it from becoming general, and in doing so, helped reorganize the conditions of human work in ways that have not stopped compounding.On a Sunday afternoon…

item.title: The Comfort of the Dashboard
item.description:
The Comfort of the DashboardOn Mistaking the Map for the TerritoryThe word comes from horse-drawn carriages.A dashboard was a protective plank mounted at the front of the carriage to deflect the mud and debris kicked up by hooves. Not a display. Not…

Read the full article on Substack

item.title: Why Gratitude Is More Demanding Than Outrage
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowOutrage is easy.It requires no patience, no memory, and very little self-restraint. It flares quickly, feels righteous, and dissipates without obligation. Outrage allows people to feel morally alert without…

item.title: The Borrowed Tune
item.description:

item.title: The World as a Feed
item.description:

item.title: The Difference Between Confidence and Conviction
item.description:
